title: trond plan output
description: Returned by `trond plan --intent ... -o json`. Preview of what apply WOULD do without performing the action.

current_state.description: Current managed-node status from state.json, or 'not_deployed' when no record exists.

desired_state.description: Always 'running'; trond's apply target is a running node.

changes.description: List of diffs between intent and state. Empty array means apply would be a no-op.

field.description: Top-level field that changed: node | intent | config | version

restart_required.description: Whether applying this change will restart the running container.

destructive.description: True if applying would destroy data (volume removal, version downgrade with incompatible state). Caller should require user confirmation.

estimated_downtime_seconds.description: Approximate seconds the node will be down during apply; 0 for no_change paths.
